title: Statistical Computing Practical 2
text:
Question 1

calc <- function(n) {
  vals <- rexp(n, 1)
  m <- mean(vals)
  v <- var(vals)
  c(m, v)
}

c1 <- makeCluster(3)
registerDoParallel(c1)

m_v <- foreach(i = 1:100, .combine = rbind) %dopar% {
  calc(n = 1000)
}

stopCluster(c1)
colnames(m_v) <- c("Mean", "Variance")
head(m_v)

              Mean  Variance
result.1 1.0172488 1.0647675
result.2 0.9853651 0.9350080
result.3 0.9799303 0.9551292
result.4 1.0165381 1.0138674
result.5 1.0164479 1.1315914
result.6 1.0094209 0.8697231

title: Statistical Computing Practical 2
Statistical Computing Practical 2
text:
Question 3

set.seed(1)

R <- 1000
B <- 1000

cl <- makeCluster(3)
registerDoParallel(cl)

hits <- foreach(r = 1:R, .combine = "+") %dopar% {
  x <- rexp(50, rate = 1)

  boot_means <- replicate(B, mean(sample(x, 50, replace = TRUE)))
  CI <- quantile(boot_means, c(0.025, 0.975), names = FALSE)

  as.integer(CI[1] <= 1 && 1 <= CI[2])
}

stopCluster(cl)

coverage <- hits / R
coverage

[1] 0.93

title: Statistical Computing Practical 2
text:
Question 4

library(iterators)

set.seed(1234)

it <- irnorm(5)   # infinite iterator of length-5 vectors

max_vals <- foreach(i = icount(3), .combine = c) %do% {
  v <- nextElem(it)
  max(v)
}

print(max_vals)

[1] 1.0844412 0.5060559 0.9594941

title: Statistical Computing Practical 2
